steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The second student claims that congestion worsened over time in ALL three areas — both smaller cities (Allentown-Bethlehem and Beaumont) AND the large city (NYC). We need data that support that universal worsening trend.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In all three urban areas shown, the amount of traffic delay per commuter per year was greater in 2000 than in 1990.: This directly confirms the second student's claim that congestion worsened everywhere — all three areas had higher delay in 2000 than 1990. ✓
  • Choice B — In at least one of the three urban areas shown, the amount of traffic delay was less than 20 hours per commuter per year at one point between 1990 and 2000.: This describes a single low data point in one area, which says nothing about a universal worsening trend across all three areas. ✗
  • Choice C — Throughout the period between 1990 and 2000, the amount of traffic delay per commuter per year was greater in the Allentown-Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, area than in the New York City, New York, area.: This compares the two areas to each other rather than showing that both worsened over time, so it doesn't support the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — In 1992, the amount of traffic delay in the New York City, New York, area was less than 30 hours per commuter per year.: This is a single data point about NYC in one year and does not show a trend across all three areas. ✗
That is the correct answer.
